hero.subtitle: How to plug Google or Facebook login into your app — one shared Code Exchange flow for web and mobile.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.description: Social login here does not put JWTs in the redirect URL. Your client starts OAuth on the API, the identity provider (IdP) handles consent, and the API returns a short-lived one-time code. Your client then exchanges that code for the same tokens you get from password login.
steps: Redirect the user to GET /api/v1/auth/{provider}?redirect=<your allowlisted URL>
User signs in with the IdP; the API callback creates or links the user
API redirects to your redirect URL with ?code=<exchangeCode> (Redis, ~60s, single use)
Your app calls POST /api/v1/auth/exchange with { code } and stores accessToken / refreshToken

enable.title: Enable a provider
enable.description: Each provider is off by default. Turn it on in .env, create an OAuth app at the IdP, then point the callback URL at this API.
items: Set the provider feature flag to true (e.g. GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED or FACEBOOK_AUTH_ENABLED)
Fill client/app credentials and CALLBACK_URL (must be the Nest API path, not your SPA)
List allowed post-login URLs in the provider REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST (web origin and/or mobile deep link)
Optionally set *_OAUTH_DEFAULT_ROLES (comma-separated Role values) for brand-new social users
Follow the provider page below for console steps and env variable names

clients.title: Integrate your client
clients.description: You only need a start URL and a callback that reads code then calls exchange. Provider pages include copy-paste samples for SPA and React Native / deep links.
items: Web: navigate (or popup) to the start endpoint; on your /auth/callback page, POST the code to /auth/exchange
Mobile: open the start URL in an in-app browser; register a deep link in the allowlist; parse code from the return URL and exchange over HTTPS
Persist tokens the same way you already do after POST /auth/login
Users created only via social login have no local password until you set one

providers.title: Choose a provider
providers.description: Pick a guide for IdP console setup, environment variables, API details, and frontend examples.

google.title: Google
google.description: Google Cloud OAuth client, env vars, endpoints, and web/mobile samples.

facebook.title: Facebook
facebook.description: Meta app + Facebook Login, env vars, endpoints, and web/mobile samples.

security.title: What you should enforce
items: Only allowlist redirects you control — open redirects are rejected
Treat exchange codes as secrets: one-time, short TTL; never log them
POST /auth/exchange is shared across providers; it returns 503 if all social providers are disabled
Existing accounts with the same verified email are linked (provider id attached; roles unchanged)

related.title: Also see
related.authJwt: Auth & JWT — password login, refresh, roles, and guards
